title: Peppermint room spray
subtitle: A fresh room spray with peppermint essential oil
excerpt: A room spray made from distilled water, vodka, and peppermint essential oil. The peppermint gives a clean, alert, cool-smelling room fragrance suited to home offices and bathrooms. Shake before each use. Do not spray near cats.
sourceNotes: Tisserand and Young, Essential Oil Safety (2nd ed., 2014): peppermint at room spray concentration. Alcohol as dispersant for EO in water-based room sprays: standard formulation practice.

content.text: A peppermint room spray gives a clean, cool, mentally alert scent that suits home offices, bathrooms, and kitchens. The

creates the characteristic cooling impression without any change in room temperature. The vodka disperses the essential oil through the water more evenly than water alone; shake the bottle before each use to redistribute the oil that floats to the top between uses.
; do not spray in rooms where cats live. Do not use on children under two years of age. Avoid spraying near open flames.

items.name: Distilled water
items.prepNote: Distilled water. Do not use tap water; the minerals will cloud the spray.

items.name: Vodka
items.prepNote: Standard vodka (40% ABV). Mix the essential oil into the vodka first.

items.name: Peppermint essential oil
items.prepNote: 3% of total spray weight. Mix into the vodka before adding the water. Toxic to cats.

content.text: Weigh the peppermint essential oil and vodka into a small dish and stir to combine. Add the distilled water and stir briefly. Using the funnel, pour into the amber glass bottle and fit the spray top. Shake gently. Label with the making date.

Shake the bottle. Hold 20 cm from the target area and mist 2 to 3 sprays into the air. The peppermint scent is sharp and dissipates more quickly than floral essential oils; 3 to 5 sprays in a medium room gives a fresh scent for approximately 20 to 30 minutes.

Store in the amber glass bottle away from heat and direct light. Shake before each use. Keeps for 6 months. Peppermint essential oil is volatile and the scent will diminish over time. Discard if any off smell or cloudiness develops.
